notes:
HGSS-era set (HeartGold & SoulSilver base, hgss1, released 2010-02-10). NO ETB in this era -> etbPacks=null. Display = 36-pack booster box.

BOOSTER STRUCTURE: 10 cards/pack per Bulbapedia Booster-pack page (HGSS/HS-era English pack = 5 commons + 3 Uncommons + 1 Reverse Holo + 1 Rare-or-better slot). Booster box = 36 packs (Bulbapedia: 'A Booster Box contains 36 booster packs'). Matches this env's hgss baseline (1 rare-or-better, ~3 uncommon, ~5 common, 1 reverse-holo -> here exactly 4/3/1/1=10). Modeled exactly: 5 commons + 3 uncommon + 1 reverse-holo + 1 rare-or-better.

AVAILABLE BUCKETS (given): {rare-holo:14, rare:21, uncommon:38, common:40, prime:6, legend:4, ultra-rare:1}. The ultra-rare (1 card) is the Shiny Gyarados (130/123), the HGSS base secret/ultra hit. The 4 'legend' bucket entries are the 4 LEGEND halves: Ho-Oh (top+bottom) and Lugia (top+bottom) — each half is its own collectible card and lands in the rare slot when pulled. The 6 primes are Ampharos, Blissey, Donphan, Feraligatr, Meganium, Typhlosion (confirmed via Bulbapedia set composition + Serebii Prime cardex).

RARE-OR-BETTER SLOT (the 1 rare-or-better card) resolves across {rare, rare-holo, prime, legend, ultra-rare}:
  - prime = 0.154 (~1:6.5 packs). Flipside Gaming Part Two: 'Primes could be obtained in roughly 1:6-7 booster packs'. Midpoint 1:6.5 -> 0.154 -> ~5.5 Primes per 36-pack box, matching PokeBeach community box reports of '5-6 Primes per box' (avg ~6 Primes + 1 secret per box; one opener reported 5 Primes).
  - legend = 0.074 (~1:13.5 packs, a LEGEND half). Flipside Part Two: 'Legends could be obtained in 1:12-18 depending on the set'; HGSS base is at the generous end of that band (only 2 LEGEND Pokemon = Ho-Oh/Lugia in the set). Using ~1:13.5 -> ~2.7 LEGEND halves per box, i.e. roughly one full LEGEND pair per box, matching the community '1-3 Legends per box' figure.
  - ultra-rare = 0.028 (~1:36 packs, ~1 per box). The Shiny Gyarados ultra is reported at ~1:36 packs (1 per booster box) in HGSS-era pull-rate discussion.
  - rare-holo = 0.333 (~1:3 packs, ~12 holos per 36-pack box). Era-convention ~1:3 holo rate (14 holos exist in the set); standard community 'regular holo rares ~12+ per box' figure (12/36=0.333).
  - rare = remainder = 1 - 0.154 - 0.074 - 0.028 - 0.333 = 0.411 (plain non-holo rare).
  Slot sums to exactly 1.0.

REVERSE-HOLO SLOT: per dataset convention (see ex-firered-leafgreen), reverse-holo filler is modeled with plain common/uncommon ids (market value is what matters). HGSS pools are near-equal (common:40 / uncommon:38), so a neutral 0.5/0.5 split is used. Sums to 1.0.

CONFIDENCE: 'low' — no per-set published pull-rate STUDY exists for this vintage set. The Prime (1:6-7), LEGEND (1:12-18) and ultra (~1:36) bands are HGSS-era community/retailer averages (Flipside Gaming aggregating PokeBeach, plus PokeBeach box-opening reports) mapped onto HGSS base, not a HGSS1-specific large sample. Vintage sealed HGSS boxes carry a heavy collector premium, so EV/booster will sit far below box price — expected; rates are NOT inflated to compensate.

INTEGRITY: every slot sums to exactly 1.0; all distribution keys {common, uncommon, rare, rare-holo, prime, legend, ultra-rare} are a strict subset of this set's available buckets. No invented per-set percentages — Prime/LEGEND/ultra bands and the ~1:3 holo convention are the sourced era figures.
